name: Gumbo - Cajun Style
recipeInstructions:
Guaranteed to conjure up dreams of moss-draped oaks and the bayou.
This was developed with the help of a Cajun friend's memory of
watching his mama cook it every Friday night when he was a boy in
Louisiana. Making a "roux" with the flour and lard is the most
important part and the secret of a good gumbo. Be careful and don't
let it burn.
1. In a heavy pan, melt the lard (shortening) over very low heat.
2. Add flour and stir continuously until it turns a tawny brown color (it may take as long as 15 minutes). Do not rush it; if it burns, the gumbo will be ruined.
3. Add onion, garlic, celery, parsley, and green pepper and braise about 5 minutes, being careful not to burn it again.
4. Add tomato puree, 1 cup water, tuna, and shrimp and simmer for 45 minutes.
5. Add file' powder and serve over fluffy steamed rice.
